If this Pokémon has 4 or more damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun’s light won’t reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

Name: Crobat
Effects: Choose Item cards or Supporter cards. During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any of the chosen cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flaps its four wings skillfully. Crobat can fly through cramped caves without needing to slow down.

Name: Absol
Effects: You may discard any number of cards from your hand until you have 4 or fewer. Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Because of this Pokémon’s ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

Name: Purrloin
Flavor-Text: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This stealthy Pokémon sneaks up behind prey without making any sound at all. It competes with Thievul for territory.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: Discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Nickit
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

Name: Thievul
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand, and then you choose 2 Energy cards you find there and shuffle them into your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

Name: Morpeko
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Move 2 [D] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

Name: Lokix
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

Name: Brute Bonnet
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has an Ancient Booster Energy Capsule attached, you may make both Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

Name: Roaring Moon ex
Effects: Knock Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon. If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out in this way, this Pokémon does 200 damage to itself.
You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Steelix
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Jirachi
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage counters from being placed on your Benched Pokémon by effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Basic Pokémon.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong.

Name: Ferrothorn
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Spinning Needles attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

Name: Durant
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your Durant.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

Name: Honedge
Flavor-Text: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

Name: Honedge
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

Name: Doublade
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike.
Flavor-Text: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

Name: Doublade
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Slicing Blade attack does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

Name: Aegislash ex

This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

Name: Zacian

During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King’s Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

Name: Skeledirge ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 60 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Orthworm
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

Name: Gholdengo ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw a card. If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw 1 more card.
Effects: Discard any number of Basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Altaria ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tatsugiri
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In recent years, this species has been very helpful in cyberspace. These Pokémon will go around checking to make sure no suspicious data exists.

Name: Porygon2
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After artificial intelligence was implemented in Porygon2, the Pokémon began using a strange language that only other Porygon2 understand.

Name: Porygon-Z
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, attach up to 4 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some say an additional program made this Pokémon evolve, but even academics can’t agree on whether Porygon-Z is really an evolution.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
This attack does 20 damage for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

Name: Miltank
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When Whismur cries, the sound of its own voice startles it, making the Pokémon cry even louder. It cries until it’s exhausted, then it falls asleep.

Name: Loudred
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The force of this Pokémon’s loud voice isn’t just the sound—it’s also the wave of air pressure that blows opponents away and damages them.

Name: Exploud
Effects: Discard your opponent’s Active Pokémon and all attached cards. If this Pokémon didn’t evolve from Loudred during this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can do more than just shout. To communicate with others of its kind, it’ll emit all sorts of sounds from the holes in its body.

Name: Spinda
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Confused and is damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Each Spinda’s spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents’ attacks brilliantly!

Name: Swablu
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

Name: Tandemaus

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, draw 2 more cards.

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from Evolution Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

Effects: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Vanillish

Name: Wimpod
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.

Flavor-Text: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.

Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

Name: Minun
Ability-Effect: If you have Plusle in play, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. The effect of Buddy Pulse doesn’t stack.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Blitzle
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

Name: Joltik
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Joltik latch on to other Pokémon and suck out static electricity. They’re often found sticking to Yamper’s hindquarters.

Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: If Tulip is in your discard pile, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
